content:
- Completed task-02 implementation: /metrics is now served from a typed benchmarkrun metrics boundary, with Prometheus-prefixed scalar gauges/counters and a runtime-backed latency histogram rendered without leaking JSON concerns into httpserver.
- Required checks passed: make check, make lint, and make test. Updated the task file to passes=true/status=done and ran .ralph/task_switch.sh successfully.
- Next after this log entry is only repository finalization: git add all files including Ralph artifacts, commit with the required task-finished message including test evidence, push, and stop.

- Read prior progress and refreshed Ralph task summaries.
- Confirmed the repository is a Go service (`go.mod`, `Makefile`, `cmd/pg_gobench`, `internal/...`) with observability work recently completed and no active bug tasks listed.
- Resolved blockers from `.ralph/current_tasks.md` against `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`: all story-05+ tasks remain blocked by unfinished predecessors, while `.ralph/tasks/smells/2026-04-30-prometheus-metrics-boundary-smells.md` is the only unblocked failing candidate and should be written to `.ralph/current_task.txt` next.

- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` with the next Ralph task path.
- Selected `.ralph/tasks/smells/2026-04-30-prometheus-metrics-boundary-smells.md` because it is the only unblocked active task with `<passes>false</passes>`; no unblocked bug tasks exist and all later story tasks are still blocked.
- Next agent context should start from that smell task and execute it, then re-run the required checks before switching tasks.

- Read previous Ralph progress and confirmed the current task pointer is `.ralph/tasks/smells/2026-04-30-prometheus-metrics-boundary-smells.md`.
- Read the required `improve-code-boundaries` and `tdd` skill instructions to follow the task protocol.
- Next I should read the task file itself, determine whether it is still in planning or execution mode, and proceed accordingly.

- Read the smell task file. It does not contain `NOW EXECUTE`, so the protocol requires creating a separate plan file and quitting after planning.
- Pulled task references showing the prior Prometheus metrics story plan and the active smell task constraints.
- Next I should inspect the current `internal/httpserver` and `internal/benchmarkrun` code to write a precise boundary-cleanup plan with TDD slices.

- Inspected the landed metrics code. `internal/httpserver` is thin, but `internal/benchmarkrun` still has duplicated snapshot concepts: JSON `Stats` and Prometheus `MetricsSnapshot`.
- Found dead code: `metricsFromResults` in `internal/benchmarkrun/metrics.go` is unused.
- Next I should inspect the runner implementation to decide whether the smell plan should collapse the dual snapshot boundary into one canonical benchmark-owned snapshot.

- Inspected `internal/benchrunner`. The runner currently derives both `benchmarkrun.Stats` and `benchmarkrun.MetricsSnapshot` from the same sampled data, confirming a duplicated public boundary.
- The likely cleanup target is to introduce one canonical benchmark-owned snapshot/view in `internal/benchmarkrun` and derive JSON results plus Prometheus exposition from it, while removing dead code and duplicated cache fields/methods.
- Next I should create the smell task plan file under a `_plans` subdir, link it from the task, mark it `TO BE VERIFIED`, and stop.

- Created the smell-task plan at `.ralph/tasks/smells/2026-04-30-prometheus-metrics-boundary-smells_plans/2026-04-30-prometheus-metrics-boundary-smells-plan.md`.
- Linked that plan from `.ralph/tasks/smells/2026-04-30-prometheus-metrics-boundary-smells.md` and left the plan ending in `TO BE VERIFIED` as required because this turn is planning-only.
- Next context should review the plan, decide whether the canonical single-snapshot boundary is correct, switch the plan ending to `NOW EXECUTE` only when satisfied, and then stop immediately or continue under execution protocol in a later turn.

- Read previous Ralph progress, the active task pointer, and both required skills (`tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries`).
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/smells/2026-04-30-prometheus-metrics-boundary-smells.md` and that the previous context already created a separate plan.
- Next I should read the task file and linked plan, then decide whether protocol requires planning-only quit or execution.

- Read the active smell task and its linked plan; the plan was still marked `TO BE VERIFIED`, so this turn stayed in planning protocol.
- Validated the plan against `internal/benchmarkrun` and `internal/benchrunner`: the existing `sampledRunStats` confirms a single canonical sample boundary is a sound refactor target, and the duplicate public `Stats`/`MetricsSnapshot` caches are the real smell.
- Switched `.ralph/tasks/smells/2026-04-30-prometheus-metrics-boundary-smells_plans/2026-04-30-prometheus-metrics-boundary-smells-plan.md` from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE`. Next context should execute that plan with strict vertical-slice TDD, then run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.

- Read saved Ralph progress plus the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skills.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/smells/2026-04-30-prometheus-metrics-boundary-smells.md` and previous context already switched its plan to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next I need to read the task file, linked plan, and current benchmark metrics code, then execute the plan with vertical-slice TDD.

- Read the active smell task, its linked plan, and the current benchmark metrics code.
- Confirmed the real boundary smell: `benchmarkrun.Run` still exposes `Snapshot()` plus `Metrics()`, `Coordinator` caches both, and `metricsFromResults` is dead.
- Next I am starting the first tracer bullet: add a failing canonical-sample test in `benchmarkrun`, then implement the new sample boundary with the smallest code needed.

- Added a new benchmark-owned `Sample` boundary in `internal/benchmarkrun` and made it the single source for JSON stats and Prometheus metrics rendering.
- Collapsed `benchmarkrun.Run`, `Coordinator`, and `benchrunner.activeRun` from `Snapshot()` plus `Metrics()` to one `Sample()` path; targeted `benchmarkrun` and `benchrunner` tests are green.
- Next I will remove any dead code left by the old dual-snapshot path, then run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.

- All required gates passed after the refactor: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.
- Next I need to update the active smell task/plan artifacts to reflect completion, run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, then commit and push all repo changes including Ralph files.
